parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-424 (RP13.P09.M09.S02-RP13.P09.M10.S03, risk A, 10 units, descriptor-only payments p09 review-closeout slice). The pack-level claim is honestly scoped: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor"; it does not over-claim production readiness and does not weaken any gate. Verification: (1) The CP424 pack binding, requirements, and no-write attestation in registry.js exactly match the baseline/manifest/plan (risk A, 10 units, range, upstream CP00-423, next CP00-425, next_subphase RP13.P09.M10.S04, deliverable distribution claude_review:3/security_audit:3/test:1/ui:1/implementation:2, micro-title 7/3). (2) closeout-pack-plan.json lists CP00-424 as the head live-open pack with matching included_units; deviation_from_plan=false. (3) service.js builders return only Object.freeze descriptors over the upstream CP423 case set; a grep across packages/payments/src for require/child_process/writeFile/fs/fetch/http/exec/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/process.env/prisma/knex returned zero matches, corroborating no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data. (4) The attestation spread chain CP392->...->CP424 is intact, so promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false propagate; contract JSON cp424_requirements and cp424_no_write_attestation mirror the registry constants, so the validate script's deepEqual assertions and the recorded exit_code 0 evidence are statically corroborated. (5) validators.js and model.test.js enforce descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false on every row, claude_is_final_approval=false, attestation flags, and handoff to CP00-425. CP178+ hardening: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection basis, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims; readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON). Claude remains a read-only reviewer, not final approver, and Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, PROGRAM_CONTRACT.descriptor_only=true), not product runtime. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected to be absent at this stage because they are produced downstream of this review run in the hardening sequence; their absence is not a defect. One non-blocking P3 inconsistency in the derived changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the pack supports closeout pending the downstream adjudication/inspection/commit steps and the standing human final approval for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-lists pack evidence dir and handoff doc as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-424/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). These two paths are pack-related (the pack's own evidence directory and its session handoff), so labeling them 'unrelated' is inaccurate. The authoritative manifest.json worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly excludes both. No functional impact on the descriptor-only/no-write boundaries, the production_ready=false claim, or coverage validation.
findings.recommendation: Remove docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-424/ from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved in changed-file-scope.json so they appear only under untracked_implementation_files, matching the manifest classification.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic/bookkeeping inconsistency in a derived scope artifact. The manifest classification is authoritative and correct; does not affect pack or goal closeout. May be corrected opportunistically.
